vspaint:finish-warning: Warning: finished paintings cannot be edited!
vspaint:brush-hint-clean: Dip in a dye source to add color.
vspaint:brush-hint-dry: Dried out! Soak in a barrel of water to clean.
vspaint:brush-hint-needbarrel: This brush is dried out. Soak it in a barrel of water to clean it.
vspaint:brush-must-wash: Wash the brush in water before dipping in a new color.
vspaint:handbook-text: Painting & Easel This mod lets you create custom pixel paintings and hang them on your walls. 1) What You'll Need Before you can start painting, craft these three items: • Painting Easel - the stand that holds your canvas while you work • Blank Canvas - the surface you paint on • Paintbrush - your painting tool You'll also need dyes to load color into your brush. Standard VS dyes work fine. Alternatively, this mod adds paint base recipes using eggs as an ingredient - check the Paint Base items for alternate dye crafting options. 2) Getting Started • Place the easel on the ground. • With a blank canvas in hand, right-click the easel to mount it. • With a wet brush in hand, right-click the easel to open the painting interface. 3) Loading Your Brush • Dip your brush in any dye source by right-clicking on it: a barrel, bucket, bowl, or even a natural water block. • The brush will show its current color in your inventory. Alternate dye colors can be produced by mixing: • Red + Blue = Purple • Red + Yellow = Orange • Blue + Yellow = Green • Blue + Green = Cyan • White + Red = Pink • Black darkens a color (Red→Dark Red, Green→Dark Green, Blue→Dark Blue, White→Dark Grey) • White lightens a color (Dark Red→Red, Dark Green→Green, Dark Blue→Blue, Dark Grey→Light Grey) • Mixing secondary colors (Orange, Purple, Green) with anything except White or Black = Brown • Brown + any color except White = Black To mix, simply dip an already-loaded brush into a different dye source. The result color will appear on the brush. 4) Painting • Open the painting interface by right-clicking the easel with a loaded brush. • Any wet brushes in your hotbar will appear as color options in the palette - load multiple brushes with different colors to paint with them all. • Select a color from the palette, choose your brush size, and click on the canvas to paint. • Use the eraser to remove strokes, undo to step back, or clear to wipe the canvas. • Use Save at any time to preserve your progress without closing. 5) Washing • To change a brush's color, dip it in water first to wash it clean, then dip in the new dye. Optional: brush drying is available as a server config setting (ModConfig/vspaint.json, EnableDryingMechanics). When enabled, a loaded brush dries out after a few hours of in-game time (configurable via DryAfterHours) and must be soaked in a barrel of water or aqua vitae before it can be used again. Drying is disabled by default. 6) Finishing Your Painting • When you're happy with the result, click Finish in the painting interface. • Warning: finished paintings cannot be edited. This is permanent. • Once finished, shift + right-click the easel to take the canvas down as a finished painting. • Hang it on any wall like a standard block. 7) How Paintings Are Stored Painting data is saved directly inside the world chunk data alongside the block itself - there are no separate files. If you share your world save, all paintings come with it automatically. Each canvas block is fully self-contained.
